calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause As To The Respondents For Order Compelling Compliance With Investigational Subpoenas And Supporting Papers
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALICE W. WONG, Esq. of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3495) for petitioner;
Atty. DEREK F. O'REILLY-JONES, Esq. of Bonne, Bridges, Mueller, O'Keefe & Nichols (Tel: 213-480-1900) for respondent.

OSC On Petition For Order Compelling Compliance With Investigational Subpoenas.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Petitioner's petition for an order compelling compliance with investigational subpoenas is granted. The Court finds that petitioner has shown good cause to order respondent to comply with the subpoenas. The Medical Board has "a compelling interest in investigating excessive or otherwise improper prescribing of controlling substances." (Cross v. Superior Court (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 305, 317.) The declaration of Sandra Karpenko, M.D. provides substantial evidence that Dr. Levitan prescribed dangerous amounts and combinations of controlled substances to numerous patients over a two-year period, which is good cause for further investigation by the Board. (Karpenko Decl. pars. 6, 16-21; see Grafilo v. Soorani (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 497, 508-512 [finding good cause to compel compliance with subpoenas based on expert report by Board's medical consultant regarding possible overprescription of controlled substances]; Cross, 11 Cal.App.5th at 327-328 [detailed declaration grounded in declarant's review of CURES reports detailing the amount of controlled substances physician prescribed in comparison to the recommended dosages "may well have sufficed by itself to establish the suspicion that would justify commencing an investigation"]; Whitney v. Montegut (2014) 222 Cal.App.4th 906, 918-920 [good cause to require compliance with subpoenas based on specific instances of doctor's prescribing irregularities].)

The cases respondent relies upon are distinguishable on the grounds that they involved far less robust factual showings. (See, e.g., Grafilo v. Cohanshohet (2019) 32 Cal.App.5th 428, 440 [declaration was insufficient to show good cause where it showed only that physician, a specialist in pain medication, prescribed high doses to five patients, but "there are no facts suggesting [physician] was negligent in treating his patients or that he prescribed controlled substances without meeting the standard of care"]; Grafilo v. Wolfsohn (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 1024, 1030, 1037 [declaration regarding five patients did not opine that data or MED level indicated by CURES report constituted a violation of the Medical Practices Act or breached a standard of care]; compare Karpenko Decl. par. 6 [CURES reports contain over 22,000 entries over two-year period, an "exceedingly large number of prescriptions and refills"]; par. 16-18 [Dr. Levitan prescribed doses to Patient 1 of 528 mg per day, nearly six times the CDC's maximum recommended dose, later increased to 672 mg per day, compared to far lower doses prescribed by a different physician]; par. 20 ["if the controlled substances prescribed by Dr. Levitan were prescribed to manage chronic pain, the doses greatly exceeded the standard of care, and have the potential for serious patient harm"].) That Dr. Karpenko is not board-certified in pain medicine does not establish that she is not competent to opine on these issues. (See Cross, 11 Cal.App.5th at 327 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in determining that specialist in internal medicine was sufficiently qualified to competently render an opinion regarding physician's controlled substance prescribing practices].) That respondent's retained expert may have reached different conclusions does not negate the Board's showing of good cause. Respondent may respond on the merits to any disciplinary proceeding initiated by the Board, once it has completed its investigation.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
